title: phryne zanonii
intro: Phryne zanonii, also known as Frangula purgans, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly referred to as the buckthorn or alder buckthorn. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purgative properties, which can be attributed to the anthraquinone glycosides present in its bark and leaves. These medicinal properties can help in treating various health issues, such as constipation, dyspepsia, and digestive problems, by stimulating bowel movements and improving digestion. Frangula purgans can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed in moderation under the guidance of a healthcare professional for its benefits to be realized.
